  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative Officer",
  "description": "O.Peenice Adio Global Enterprise, based in Lagos, Nigeria, is a registered business specializing in thesales, supply, and distribution of general goods, general merchandise, and general contracting services. Registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC), the enterprise operates in areas such as manufacturing, warehousing, and sales executive roles.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage office supplies stock and place orders
	Prepare regular reports on expenses and office budgets
	Maintain and update company databases
	Organize a filing system for important and confidential company documents
	Answer queries by employees and clients
	Update office policies as needed
	Maintain a company calendar and schedule appointments
	Book meeting rooms as required
	Distribute and store correspondence (e.g. letters, emails and packages)
	Prepare reports and presentations with statistical data, as assigned
	Arrange travel and accommodations
	Schedule in-house and external events


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as an Administrative Officer, Administrator or similar role
	Solid knowledge of office procedures
	Experience with office management software like MS Office (MS Excel and MS Word, specifically)
	Strong organization skills with a problem-solving attitude
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Attention to detail.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 / month.

Method ofApplication&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hropeeniceglobent@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Administrative Officer Job at O.Peenice Adio Global Enterprise




        
    

        
        O.Peenice Adio Global Enterprise, based in Lagos, Nigeria, is a registered business specializing in thesales, supply, and distribution of general goods, general merchandise, and general contracting services. Registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC), the enterprise operates in areas such as manufacturing, warehousing, and sales executive roles.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage office supplies stock and place orders
	Prepare regular reports on expenses and office budgets
	Maintain and update company databases
	Organize a filing system for important and confidential company documents
	Answer queries by employees and clients
	Update office policies as needed
	Maintain a company calendar and schedule appointments
	Book meeting rooms as required
	Distribute and store correspondence (e.g. letters, emails and packages)
	Prepare reports and presentations with statistical data, as assigned
	Arrange travel and accommodations
	Schedule in-house and external events


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as an Administrative Officer, Administrator or similar role
	Solid knowledge of office procedures
	Experience with office management software like MS Office (MS Excel and MS Word, specifically)
	Strong organization skills with a problem-solving attitude
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Attention to detail.


Salary
N120,000 - N150,000 / month.

Method ofApplication 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hropeeniceglobent@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant",
  "description": "Ikeja Electricity Distribution Company (IKEDC) -&nbsp;Buoyed by a mission to redefine customer experience and be the provider of choice wherever energy is consumed, Ikeja Electric Plc, Nigeria&rsquo;s largest power distribution network powers lives and businesses with innovation and unwavering drive for excellence. The company began its new phase of growth and expansion on November 1st, 2013 following the handover of the defunct Power Holding Company Of Nigeria (PHCN) to NEDC/ KEPCO Consortium under the privatization scheme of the Federal Government of Nigeria.

The consortium has the Korean Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) which generates about 84,000MW in capacity and has a global efficiency record of a maximum down time period of slightly above three minutes annually as technical partners.

This partnership has positioned IE to effectively drive its commitment to deliver efficient and sustainable power supply through investments in new technology, infrastructure upgrade and human capital development.

Ikeja Electric has over 1,000,000 customers who the company is committed to serve with a New Spirit, New Drive and New Energy. This resolve continues to elicit a passion for service excellence and new thinking on how to empower lives and businesses across the IE network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos Mainland, Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Power Sector

Job Description


	We are currently recruiting for an Executive Assistant to the CEO for a reputable company in the power sector.
	The ideal candidate will provide high-level administrative and strategic support to the CEO while ensuring smooth coordination of executive activities and priorities.


Requirements


	Minimum of 5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant
	Must currently be working as an Executive Assistant (or similar role) supporting a CEO/Managing Director
	Must reside within Lagos Mainland
	Strong ability to manage executive schedules, priorities, and communication


Required Technical Skills:


	Data Analysis
	Research
	Microsoft Office Proficiency
	Project Management


Key Competencies:


	Excellent organizational and time-management skills
	Strong communication and stakeholder management
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion
	Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment


Salary
N800,000 - N1,500,000 / month.&nbsp;",

Executive Assistant Job at Ikeja Electricity Distribution Company (IKEDC)




        
    

        
        Ikeja Electricity Distribution Company (IKEDC) - Buoyed by a mission to redefine customer experience and be the provider of choice wherever energy is consumed, Ikeja Electric Plc, Nigeria’s largest power distribution network powers lives and businesses with innovation and unwavering drive for excellence. The company began its new phase of growth and expansion on November 1st, 2013 following the handover of the defunct Power Holding Company Of Nigeria (PHCN) to NEDC/ KEPCO Consortium under the privatization scheme of the Federal Government of Nigeria.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



The consortium has the Korean Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) which generates about 84,000MW in capacity and has a global efficiency record of a maximum down time period of slightly above three minutes annually as technical partners.

This partnership has positioned IE to effectively drive its commitment to deliver efficient and sustainable power supply through investments in new technology, infrastructure upgrade and human capital development.

Ikeja Electric has over 1,000,000 customers who the company is committed to serve with a New Spirit, New Drive and New Energy. This resolve continues to elicit a passion for service excellence and new thinking on how to empower lives and businesses across the IE network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant

Job Location: Lagos Mainland, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Power Sector

Job Description


	We are currently recruiting for an Executive Assistant to the CEO for a reputable company in the power sector.
	The ideal candidate will provide high-level administrative and strategic support to the CEO while ensuring smooth coordination of executive activities and priorities.


Requirements


	Minimum of 5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant
	Must currently be working as an Executive Assistant (or similar role) supporting a CEO/Managing Director
	Must reside within Lagos Mainland
	Strong ability to manage executive schedules, priorities, and communication


Required Technical Skills:


	Data Analysis
	Research
	Microsoft Office Proficiency
	Project Management


Key Competencies:


	Excellent organizational and time-management skills
	Strong communication and stakeholder management
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion
	Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment


Salary
N800,000 - N1,500,000 / month.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Recovery Officer",
  "description": "ICS Outsourcing Limited is Nigeria&rsquo;s leading Outsourcing Company and Business Support Solutions provider. We offer a comprehensive range of outsourcing services ranging from People Outsourcing to Sales BPO to all kinds and sizes of business. Value added services to our client&rsquo;s has is our desire. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing our client&rsquo;s expectations. We are one of the Top indigenous Companies in Nigeria to work for. Outsourcing in Nigeria, Outsource To Nigeria, Top Companies in Nigeria, Nigeria, outsourcing company in Nigeria, top outsourcing company in Nigeria, top, list of outsourcing company in Nigeria, outsourcing firms in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Recovery Officer
JLocation:&nbsp;Ketu / Mile 12, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Field Recovery Officer is responsible for recovering outstanding debts from delinquent customers through field visits, negotiations, and structured repayment arrangements.
	The role involves maintaining positive customer relationships while ensuring compliance with company policies and regulatory guidelines.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate the activities of Field Verification Officers across the state.
	Ensure all verifications are completed in a timely and accurate manner.
	Escalate operational issues proactively and support prompt resolution.
	Conduct physical visits to business premises to evaluate financial operations, inventory, and overall business health.
	Carry out on-site verifications of residential and business addresses.
	Visit guarantors and referees to confirm their awareness and availability in case of future contact.
	Compile and submit detailed daily reports on all verification activities.
	Perform other relevant duties as assigned by your supervisor.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in any relevant discipline.
	A minimum of 2 years of experience in credit risk or field verification is an added advantage
	1&ndash;3 years of experience in debt recovery, credit control, or field collections (preferably in financial services, microfinance, or FMCG).
	In-depth geographical knowledge of the local environment
	Apt attention to details
	Proven simple/complex analytical skills
	Excellent communication, presentation and interpersonal skills
	Agility and perseverance


Key Competencies:


	Negotiation &amp; Conflict Resolution
	Target-driven &amp; Result-oriented
	Integrity &amp; Professionalism
	Strong Problem-Solving Skills
	Resilience and Ability to Handle Pressure.


Salary
N201,000 monthly.

Mode of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send&nbsp;their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;aawosanya@icsoutsourcing.com&nbsp;using &quot;Field Recovery Officer&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Field Recovery Officer Job at ICS Outsourcing Limited




        
    

        
        ICS Outsourcing Limited is Nigeria’s leading Outsourcing Company and Business Support Solutions provider. We offer a comprehensive range of outsourcing services ranging from People Outsourcing to Sales BPO to all kinds and sizes of business. Value added services to our client’s has is our desire. We have a reputation for satisfying and surpassing our client’s expectations. We are one of the Top indigenous Companies in Nigeria to work for. Outsourcing in Nigeria, Outsource To Nigeria, Top Companies in Nigeria, Nigeria, outsourcing company in Nigeria, top outsourcing company in Nigeria, top, list of outsourcing company in Nigeria, outsourcing firms in Nigeria.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Recovery Officer
JLocation: Ketu / Mile 12, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Field Recovery Officer is responsible for recovering outstanding debts from delinquent customers through field visits, negotiations, and structured repayment arrangements.
	The role involves maintaining positive customer relationships while ensuring compliance with company policies and regulatory guidelines.


Key Responsibilities


	Supervise and coordinate the activities of Field Verification Officers across the state.
	Ensure all verifications are completed in a timely and accurate manner.
	Escalate operational issues proactively and support prompt resolution.
	Conduct physical visits to business premises to evaluate financial operations, inventory, and overall business health.
	Carry out on-site verifications of residential and business addresses.
	Visit guarantors and referees to confirm their awareness and availability in case of future contact.
	Compile and submit detailed daily reports on all verification activities.
	Perform other relevant duties as assigned by your supervisor.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of HND / B.Sc. in any relevant discipline.
	A minimum of 2 years of experience in credit risk or field verification is an added advantage
	1–3 years of experience in debt recovery, credit control, or field collections (preferably in financial services, microfinance, or FMCG).
	In-depth geographical knowledge of the local environment
	Apt attention to details
	Proven simple/complex analytical skills
	Excellent communication, presentation and interpersonal skills
	Agility and perseverance


Key Competencies:


	Negotiation & Conflict Resolution
	Target-driven & Result-oriented
	Integrity & Professionalism
	Strong Problem-Solving Skills
	Resilience and Ability to Handle Pressure.


Salary
N201,000 monthly.

Mode of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: aawosanya@icsoutsourcing.com using "Field Recovery Officer" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Inventory Manager",
  "description": "Demmyrayo Ventures is a dynamic, customer-focused business committed to delivering quality products and reliable services across multiple sectors. We operate with integrity, professionalism, and a strong dedication to meeting client needs while creating long-term value for our partners and stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.


Requirements and Skills


	BSc/BA in Business Administration, Logistics or relevant field;
	Relevant qualifications
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;hrdemmyrayoentltd@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Inventory Manager Job at Demmyrayo Ventures




        
    

        
        Demmyrayo Ventures is a dynamic, customer-focused business committed to delivering quality products and reliable services across multiple sectors. We operate with integrity, professionalism, and a strong dedication to meeting client needs while creating long-term value for our partners and stakeholders.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Devise ways to optimize inventory control procedures
	Inspect the levels of business supplies and raw material to identify shortages
	Ensure product stock is adequate for all distribution channels and can cover direct demand from customers
	Record daily deliveries and shipments to reconcile inventory
	Use software to monitor demand and document characteristics of inventory
	Place orders to replenish stock avoiding insufficiencies or excessive surplus
	Analyze data to anticipate future needs
	Evaluate suppliers to achieve cost-effective deals and maintain trust relationships
	Collaborate with warehouse employees and other staff to ensure business goals are met
	Report to upper management on stock levels, issues etc.


Requirements and Skills


	BSc/BA in Business Administration, Logistics or relevant field;
	Relevant qualifications
	Proven experience as inventory manager or similar position
	Excellent knowledge of data analysis and forecasting methods
	Working knowledge of inventory management software (e.g. ERP)
	Ability to accurately track inventory and create reports
	An analytical mind with strong math skills
	Excellent organizational and planning skills
	Outstanding communication and interpersonal abilities
	Reliable and trustworthy


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hrdemmyrayoentltd@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Marketer Job at ITM Services Limited




        
    

        
        ITM Services Limited, established as the Nigerian cornerstone of ITM Group, delivers unparalleled expertise in human resource solutions, business process outsourcing, and supply chain management. With strategic offices in Abuja and Lagos, we provide uniquely professional approaches to Human Capital Management that align with business strategies and industry best practices, creating exceptional value through your most vital asset—your people.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our focus is on helping organizations enhance their performance by equipping them with the right tools and talent to foster growth and remain competitive in today’s dynamic business environment. Through our services, we support businesses in developing their workforce, improving workplace culture, and driving sustainable success. At ITM Nigeria, we take pride in our commitment to excellence, integrity, and client satisfaction. Our reputation for delivering high-value services has earned us the trust of businesses seeking to thrive in Nigeria’s competitive marketplace.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Marketer 

Job Locations: Minna - Niger, Zamfara, Kebbi, Yobe, Borno, Kastina, Kano, Adamawa, Benue
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Minimum qualification: B.Sc in any discipline
	Must have 1-2 years of experience of sales and marketing 
	Must have prior experience in a marketing role in the Pensions industry.
	Must have good communication and interpersonal skills
	Proficient in basic computer usage (Microsoft Office Suite)
	Must be customer-focused and professional in conduct
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision
	Drive business growth.


Salary
N100,000 - N250,000 / month.
